dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a breathing problem in the city of Richmond.
Hi, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I called because of left-sided chest and flank pain. I also have left-sided weakness and a headache on the left.
Karen, what is your name and where are we?
I'm Karen, and we're in Richmond.
Can you squeeze my fingers with each hand as hard as you can?
Yes, here goes.
Left arm drift is noted, and the grip is weaker on the left.
Karen, can you tell me which finger I'm pointing to?
Radial pulse is 62, strong and regular.
Your airway appears clear. Any difficulty speaking or breathing?
No, I can speak and breathe fine.
Breathing is regular and unlabored-20 respirations per minute.
Are you currently short of breath?
I had some, but it improves when I calm down.
Skin is warm, dry, and normal in color. No pallor or cyanosis noted.
Blood pressure is 114 over 61, and it feels stable.
Oxygen saturation is 100% on room air, consistent with baseline measurements.
Blood sugar is 135 mg/dL-within normal range.
When did your symptoms begin?
It started yesterday morning, while I was sleeping. I don't know the exact time it started.
Was it a sudden or gradual onset?
It was sudden. I woke up with it, and the pain has been there since then.
Were you doing anything when it began?
I was just resting or sleeping when it began. No activity or stress associated with it.
How would you describe the pain quality?
It's a deep, pulling pain on the left side, into the left flank and back area.
Does it radiate elsewhere?
Yes, the pain travels into my left back region.
On a scale from 0 to 10, how bad is the pain?
It's a 10-the worst possible. It is intermittent, though, not constant all the time.
Any existing medical conditions or surgeries we should know about?
I have a history of hypothyroidism. I also had breast surgery in the past, but not recent. Otherwise, nothing else major.
Do you have any known allergies such as medications or foods?
No known drug or food allergies. I was made aware of that before I started medication treatment long ago, like levothyroxine.
When was your last meal or drink, and were you feeling unwell at the time?
I don't remember the exact time, but I wasn't feeling sick when I ate or drank anything after the symptoms started.
Are you seeing any new or unusual rashes, scars, bites, marks, or skin discoloration besides what you mentioned already about the left side?
No, I don't have any bruising or rashes. I have an old scar from previous breast surgery, but no new injuries or marks noted on the left side or anywhere else that's unusual.

I'm going to check your eyes and head for any changes in response or visual function as part of our standard protocol for stroke signs-changing eye pupils or visual disturbances? Also, left arm weakness is a noted concern so we need to fully document our findings accordingly with your condition matched to our standard form called Cincinnati stroke scale.

Pupils are equal, round, and reactive to light. No droop found. Left arm drift is confirmed. Grip is achieved with the right, not with the left. Facial symmetry is normal and speech is clear and audible.

Are you aware of your surroundings and the current time and place? Were you aware this was happening to you yesterday or since it started and was checked before at a recent visit at St. Mary's hospital but unsure of the result or outcome of that exam you said earlier when we met you while you were outside, not from trauma or falls according to you.

Yes, I knew the moment it started, and I also had it checked at St. Mary's yesterday. I didn't remember what they told me from that time, but I did go there and let them check the symptoms and left from there with an appointment or whatever, not due to any recent accident or trauma it's all been left to the doctors to figure out until now and for the eve of this call out for help here now.

Did you have any recent confusion, memory loss, or new symptoms besides what we're already going over about the left side pain and left hand weakness? This all features strongly in the stroke-probability screening, so we have to confirm behavioral normality conditions with that being one of our main research tools now in our protocols we follow step by step before prescribing either tool or set of steps taken via our methods here each week on team rotation shifts until a better decision is made from all data gathered in case there's a critical condition rise from both system scan and patient statement input added together for proper response route start and quick call outs if needed.

No confusion. I stayed oriented and aware all through. I did get tested at St. Mary's, but I didn't get the results, and nothing was really discussed beyond that usually unless it's critical or complicated enough to call something like that today, but in my case, so far, I'm stable enough at view and talk time now and not any danger zone until someone made that shift into higher threat calls us all to active response.

We are placing EKG pads all over so we can start checking heart rhythms, signs of heart damage, and possible issues with the sinus node. Is that okay with you, both from skin and pressure experience sides while we run this throughout your left, right, and upper body regions carefully before action sets move on unless problems arise later in that specific area or in the overall long check phase here with electronic tools now added for every use case scenarios this way now handled faster with less mistakes when maintenance is done correctly via proper shifts and training sessions each week before going into difficult or stressful units to handle, so equipment is reliable when we report what we find in case there's a cardiac trigger or otherwise complicating element to this case here today via unlikely but still dangerous triggers with no time to react effectively otherwise if no equipment needs backup support or prompt replacement even before that time slot sets in by pure luck or shift randomness now all handled before team sends arrive now also.

Yes, that's okay with me. Please do whatever it is you need to check here for health and strong operation tracking before leaving with full team coverage so I can be taken quickly and safely without delay via whatever hospital is best suited for detected conditions and is ready for patient arrivals quickly after response times so nothing is wasted in sending me somewhere else that can't do the work being needed directly here as part of the same timeline I started with at phone call and thereby ending what could be dangerous delays via bad coordination or missed conditions before emergency needs that are too late or worse gets missed entirely.

EKG shows sinus bradycardia with no acute changes beyond normal baselines. No signs of heart attack or ischemic signs detected even during first current wave checks we found at the start of this EKG reading and ongoing check runs we did here at this shift, so nothing seems to be worsening except potential hidden post-disease onset stage triggers we will see in another run if any arise after first check but no signs there now the best we can tell via system updates now available for rapid use and readiness beyond single readups and wait times between them unless an evolving scenario triggers in real time during continuous data capture as per protocols set by default when equipment activates upon proper setup and check runs with no built faults in stored case data time slots with no corruption or missing data unavoidably while in use, so reading can be trusted unless fails in real time at next steps to be escalated further up from original readings taken at baseline before any changes occur here today via possible issues from patient side or system errors signaled in return data known before as bad indicators of trust worthiness now all accounted for by now if they are seen and saved or stored for later action and blame free shifts unless timeout or errors show up in logs during or after next checks until operator calls time out for further review at station data boards or similar systems where possible, but so far, nothing is marked as defective or dangerous here so we can proceed for now<>();

12-lead EKG shows possible left atrial enlargement and minimal anterior infarction or ischemia. No clear ST segment changes or signs of heart attack as of the initial scan, but we noted a small overall QRS voltage in the chest leads in these and all checks done so far, showing no major heart damage beyond basic aging or other long-term issues rather than acute emergencies that would pop up from sudden events as per this shift's protocols setup and notes already taken before next steps or further signs arise here today via internal data readings now in full functionality mode here.

GCS findings remain at 15-patient is alert, oriented, and has no confusion, with full recall of time, place, and current status. Stroke signs are noted via the Cincinnati stroke scale due to left arm drift and arm grip strength difference, but no facial droop or slurred speech observed in this case, which keeps stroke probability low but still requires protocol map 3-5 being actively used and followed based on guidelines we know apply here to our work settings and prescribed response loading screens now active until patient arrival at proper facility listed as VCU for our next steps and transfer case--

Any injuries or issues with your legs or back? This will help us fully assess for signs of trauma or infarction to left limb, characterization of lower joint or muscle irregularities according to standard musculoskeletal exams given for mobility control and pressure needs. Also, we will check for any instability or other problems in the pelvis area with skin related to reproductive conditions or walking irregularity given her age and mobility issues that were noted either way, to see current viability for walking later or if any transfer is needed for non-support stance conditions unless already set to take with each move counted for as per upcoming actions--

No, I don't have any deformities or injuries in those areas. I do have a small scar on my lower back, but no pain or issue with leg movement or balance worsening beyond initial call, although there is some lack of confidence when I attempt to move forward without crutches or even one helped hand to support me here, so we might need a second check for this as well unless the condition resolves itself from random fluctuation periods or the toughness frequency problems arise from initial Checks being done here.

Given the findings of left arm drift, subtle cardiology findings, and alert status, we are following protocol 3-5 for stroke, TIA, and related transient issues. While symptoms are not fully consistent with major motor deficits or speech changes, the left-sided weakness and asymmetric grip suggest activation of this protocol for closer monitoring and treatment association after possible transfer to ED for CT scan and MRI if needed after hospital set-ups and team stands protocol agreed to be active for each call in these cases as per primary roll-up document tracking--

We will set up an IV in your right arm-20-gauge needle. This is a fairly standard practice for hydration, medication access, and clear line to give liquid treatments when required from home or hospital calls. You will be given a small amount of saline while in transport system for baseline stability efforts and future drug access ability in case further responses are required at any point in care transfer chain as part of this shift or future shifts--

That's fine. I do not have any issues with needles, but I am a little stiff there-please go slowly and report progress so we can know if delicate area tweaking is needed or if something pops up from typical insertion pain side as per signage here--

IV inserted in right antecubital-20G line in. All checks show correct vascular insertion and no bleeding risk detected unless subsequent pressure from moves or tension manipulation causes another blood issue later, but for now, there are no issues in that site. No reported pain or fluctuation detected to cause current attention step--

Administering 10 mL normal saline flush. This will ensure that the IV line remains clear and functional for any future drug treatments that might become necessary as part of pre-hospital care or upon arrival at emergency departments or other transit set-ups in hospital chain. No further action needed unless more symptoms arise that need rapid intervention--
Pulse now at 52-becoming slower than earlier assessment. This matches the EKG findings for sinus bradycardia in this patient form(case#

Respirations increased to 33 per minute. That is expected in some cases of health stress signals being sent from brain or body organ dysfunction, especially during some of the mental stress stages I see occurring in some patients during our verbal engagement process--

Blood pressure is now 108 over 72. Still stable but trending lower as vitals run, but nothing indicating emergency drop unless there is an unknown internal drop through monitoring that will activate sensors later during check--
Oxygen saturation remains on line at 97% via device readings and ambient oxygen recalculations here, so no change in respiratory support needed at the moment--

Blood glucose is now zero and unobtainable from current clamps or device testing. We are likely seeing a failure in system or sensor readings here, but no clear cause has been found for this sudden change unless we get an add-on checkup later--

Is the pain still 10/10 and in the same area? Any changes observed since we started with our use of tools to help assess this case within our backup plans unless something much bigger is occurring over time--
Yes, still on the left and radiating to the back. No improvement or worsening that we see as yet here until current tools are applied or movements are made elsewhere to check further--
No additional procedures or medications at this time. We maintain current monitoring and IV system advancement for any possible future requirement--

We'll be transporting you to VCU Emergency Department-this is the appropriate facility based on protocol we have in effect and past experience with them handling such cases rapidly with specialized neurology and cardiology equipment--
I'll secure you with a 5-point harness for safe transport. This is standard for neuro or critical movement cases where balance instability or other factors could lead to fall risks during transport--
Be gentle-I have balance issues. I take this seriously for long term issues and need special attention here until secure--
Transferring a 71-year-old female with left-sided chest and flank pain, left-sided weakness, left-sided asymmetric grip, and left-sided headache--
Vital signs now include BP 108/72, pulse 52 with sinus bradycardia, and previously measured blood glucose of 135 now reading zero, possibly device malfunction--
20G IV and 10 mL saline administered. Stroke protocol initiated per Cincinnati test findings for left-sided weakness found here--
No emergency transport requested by command center or based on current situation-transport is non-emergent to VCU for further workup--
